name: Lumpia (Close Kin to Chi.egg Rools)
recipeIngredient: 1/3 lb Med. size raw shrimp, shelled, deveined, chopped
12 Lumpia wrappers (recipe) or egg roll skins (thicker than homemade lumpia, so not as delicate a crust after frying)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare sweet and sour sauce; set aside.
2. Prepare lumpia wrappers and set aside.
3. Soak mushrooms in warm water to cover for 30 minutes, then drain. Cut off and discard stems; finely chop caps.
4. Crumble pork into a wide frying pan. Add onions and garlic and cook over medium-high heat, stirring until meat is browned (about 6 minutes).
5. Add shrimp, mushrooms, and water chestnuts and cook for 2 minutes. Stir in soy sauce. Let cool; then discard excess pan juices.
6. Fill and fold lumpia, starting from a corner diagonally, then roll up bringing the sides in, moistening wrapper edges with egg to seal. (You may cover and refrigerate for up to 8 hours at this point.)
7. Pour oil into a 2 to 3 quart pan to a depth of 1-1/2 inches and heat to 340°F on a deep frying thermometer. (If using egg rolls, heat to 360°F.) Add 4 or 5 lumpia; cook, turning as needed until golden brown (2-3 minutes).
8. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels; keep warm in a 200°F oven.
9. Repeat with remaining lumpia.
10. Serve with sweet & sour sauce.
